medications.indication_rationale: Targets the inflammatory sunburn pain; source advised 400mg with food up to three times daily for 48–72h.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 400mg orally with food up to three times daily for 48–72h (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Adjunct analgesia; source advised 1000mg up to four times daily.
medications.dose_route_frequency: 1000mg orally up to four times daily (max 4g/day) (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: bland fragrance-free emollient / aloe vera gel (refrigerated)
medications.indication_rationale: Soothes and cools; apply several times daily.

medications.drug_name: ice applied directly / topical anaesthetic sprays (e.g. benzocaine)
medications.indication_rationale: Ice worsens injury; topical anaesthetic sprays risk sensitisation — avoid.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Cool (not icy) compresses / a damp cool t-shirt for comfort; drink extra fluids; strictly avoid further sun until healed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Cooling and hydration relieve symptoms; sun avoidance prevents worsening.

patient_education_points.point: This is a superficial sunburn (first-degree) — only the top layer of skin. It peaks around 24 hours then settles and peels over days. Anti-inflammatory pain relief, cool compresses, moisturiser and fluids are the mainstays; no ice.
Drink extra fluids and stay out of the sun completely until it has healed and peeled.

Go to ED if large blisters form or the burn is very extensive, or if you develop a severe headache with confusion, vomiting, dizziness/faintness, a very high temperature, or you stop passing urine (possible heat illness/dehydration).

follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Blistering/extensive burn or heat-illness features — ED.

must_include_items: NSAID + paracetamol + cool compress/emollient + hydration; no ice
errors_of_commission: Missing heat stroke / an extensive or blistering burn
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires depth assessment, symptomatic care with hydration (no ice), and a heat-illness/deeper-burn safety-net.
